ad.title: Project Manager (Built Environment)
ad.description:
Job Duties and Responsibilities

  • Work with cross-functional teams of Technical Officers and Managers to oversee project works involving MEP, building, structural, civil works in an Integrated Facilities Management (IFM) Contract and ensure that KPIs are met
  • Responsible for preliminary survey, feasibility studies and working with consultants, vendors, users to determine requirements, preparing cost estimates to derive project budgets for various multi-disciplinary projects.
  • Ensure that project technical specifications, regulatory compliances, code of practises, schedule of work and all necessary documentations are obtained, and project milestones are met.
  • Responsible for developing detailed project plans, budgets, schedule, ensure that project technical specifications, regulatory compliances, code of practises, all necessary documentations are obtained, project milestones are met and ensuring high quality workmanship are delivered.
  • Attend regular maintenance meetings on behalf of the Company with Clients and Clients’ Representatives.
  • Periodic review for on-going project to identify risks, verifying stages of work, present reports, post-project evaluation and finalize completion of works.


Required Qualifications

  • At least 5 years relevant working experience in Project Management works involving engineering / A&A projects.
  • Recognized Degree in Building/Mechanical/Electrical Engineering, Construction Management, Project Management, Facilities Management, Quantity Surveyor or equivalent.
  • Strong understanding of M&E systems, building infrastructure, and maintenance best practices and ability to read / interpret technical drawings, schematics.
  • Excellent leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills
  • Knowledge in BIM, AutoCAD and/or Revit MEP advantageous.

ad.title: Maintenance Engineer (Security System)
ad.description:
Roles & Responsibilities

Key Roles and Responsibilities

  • To troubleshoot and maintain/service security systems at clients’ premises
  • Attend to fault calls during/after office hours
  • Support Maintenance Lead to upkeep Clients’ security system inventory list and maintenance spares
  • Support Maintenance Lead to liaise with clients, integrated facility management representatives and trade contractors so as to facilitate to carry out work at clients’ premises
  • Liaise closely with clients, integrated facility management representatives on all service/maintenance related issues to achieve optimal results.
  • Ensure that works carried out are in compliance with the Workplace Safety and Health Act.
  • Perform any other technical tasks as required including new installations, A&A, HOTO, T&C and equipment replacement etc

Requirements

  • Diploma/Higher Nitec/Nitec in Engineering/IT or relevant working experience in the related field
  • Hands on experience in security systems such as Integrated Security Management Systems, Card Access Control Systems, Intrusion Detection Systems, CCTV systems and Electronic Key Management System.
  • Additional knowledge in hardware configuration, System Networking and integration with third-party systems will be an advantage
  • Must be a team player,Independent and willing to take ownership and Pro-active in Problem solving
  • Driving License (Car or Motorbike) – Preferably if candidate possesses its own vehicle
  • Singapore Citizens are preferred
  • Work Location is at 3 Shan Road/Clients’ premises
  • 5 days work week
  • Require to perform rotational standby duties
  • Experience Candidate will be considered for Maintenance Engineer
  • Fresh Graduates keen to seek a career path in security system are welcome to apply as a Maintenance Technician

ad.title: Accounts Clerk
ad.description:
Roles & Responsibilities

Responsibilities

  • Record daily financial transactions, including accounts payable invoices and general ledger journal entries, accurately and promptly in the accounting system.
  • Prepare fixed assets schedule. 
  • Generate monthly inventory reports and maintain accurate inventory records.
  • Liaise with internal teams and external parties to resolve accounting discrepancies.
  • Other duties as assigned by the Superior

Requirements

  • Diploma in Accounting

  • Good knowledge of Microsoft Office Excel and Words; able to work independently with minimum supervision and meet deadlines

ad.title: Technician
ad.description:
Key Responsibilities

  • Troubleshooting & Repair: Diagnose mechanical, electrical, and hydraulic system failures (pumps, motors, valves, actuators) and perform corrective actions.
  • Maintenance: Perform routine preventative maintenance, including oil changes, filter replacements, oiling, and greasing of machines.
  • Technical Inspection: Inspect equipment for leaks, wear, or damage and conduct tests to ensure functionality.
  • Component Overhaul: Overhaul hydraulic components such as pumps, cylinders, and motors.
  • Safety Compliance: Execute lockout/tag-out (LOTO) procedures and comply with Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) regulations.
  • Documentation: Maintain accurate service records, maintenance logs, and job status reports.


Requirements & Qualifications

  • Education: NITEC/Diploma in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or related fields.
  • Experience: 1–3+ years of relevant experience in hydraulic, diesel machinery, or heavy equipment maintenance.
  • Skills: Proficiency with hand/power tools, ability to read hydraulic/electrical circuit diagrams, and knowledge of troubleshooting
  • Physical Ability: Must be capable of working in workshops, shipyards, or on-site, sometimes in demanding conditions
Location
  • ​80 Lim Chu Kang Lane 1
null
null
null
null

ad.title: Senior Mechanical Design Engineer – Innovative Assembly Solutions
ad.description:
About the Role

  • Design and develop mechanical components and assemblies to address technical challenges at both sub-assembly and full-system levels.
  • Create detailed drawings for manufacturing parts, ensuring clarity and precision for production teams.
  • Translate customer specifications into comprehensive technical requirements through thorough testing and analysis.
  • Assess potential risks associated with proposed solutions to recommend the most reliable options.
  • Generate detailed documentation including design reports, test summaries, and related technical files.
  • Assist in the fabrication process, including welding, machining, and assembly activities, to ensure quality standards are maintained.
  • Perform structural evaluations on components and assemblies to verify compliance with design criteria.
  • Select appropriate materials, actuators, fasteners, and other components to fulfill design specifications.
  • Design hydraulic systems incorporating pumps, valves, cylinders, hoses, and safety mechanisms.
  • Apply engineering principles creatively to develop innovative solutions that meet project objectives.
  • Develop prototypes and proof-of-concept models to validate design concepts.

Qualifications & Experience

  • A bachelor's degree in mechanical engineering or a related field.
  • At least two years of experience in mechanical component design or related roles.
  • Proficiency with CAD tools, particularly SolidWorks, for detailed modeling and drafting.
  • Solid understanding of sheet metal fabrication, weldments, truss structures, and machine parts.
  • Knowledge of design for manufacturability, assembly, and ingress protection standards.
  • Experience with motion control systems and kinematic analysis.
  • Familiarity with finite element analysis (FEA) under static loading conditions.
  • Additional expertise in hydraulics, mobile systems, or vehicle-mounted equipment is advantageous.

What We Offer

  • Opportunities to work on cutting-edge projects that challenge your engineering skills.
  • A collaborative environment that fosters innovation and continuous learning.
  • Competitive compensation packages aligned with industry standards.
  • Flexible work arrangements to support work-life balance.
  • Access to professional development resources and training programs.

ad.title: Senior Quality Assurance Specialist for Robotics & Automation Projects
ad.description:
Join Our Client as a Quality Assurance Specialist in Robotics and Vehicle Technologies

Our Client is seeking a dedicated QA professional to uphold the highest standards of product excellence across innovative robotics, automation, and specialized vehicle initiatives. This role emphasizes practical involvement in testing, analyzing, and refining products to ensure they align with rigorous quality benchmarks.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design and execute comprehensive testing strategies: Develop detailed test plans and carry out systematic evaluations of components and finished products to verify compliance with quality criteria.
  • Implement testing procedures: Establish and follow protocols for different phases of product development, utilizing both manual inspections and automated testing tools.
  • Detect and address issues: Spot defects or irregularities in products or processes, working closely with development teams to understand specifications and embed quality considerations throughout the lifecycle.
  • Contribute to ongoing enhancements: Engage in initiatives aimed at boosting product quality and manufacturing efficiency by analyzing feedback and data trends.
  • Maintain detailed documentation: Record testing methodologies, results, and findings accurately, preparing comprehensive reports that adhere to industry standards and internal policies.

ad.title: Electrical Systems Engineer – Power Electronics & Control Panel Design
ad.description:
Join Our Client as an Electrical Systems Specialist

We are seeking a dedicated professional to become a key member of our electrical engineering team, focusing on innovative electronics and electrical solutions. In this role, you will be instrumental in designing control panels, developing power electronic circuits, and working on battery-powered device applications. Your expertise in electrical integration will be vital to project success.

The position involves close collaboration with cross-disciplinary teams, requiring proactive communication and coordination. You will be responsible for delivering high-quality work within established timelines and budgets, ensuring project milestones are met efficiently.

Core Responsibilities

  • Initiate early-stage feasibility assessments to guide project development
  • Translate client needs into detailed electrical specifications
  • Design and develop electrical schematics, control panels, wiring harnesses, and cable looms
  • Create detailed instructions for manufacturing and assist in resolving production challenges
  • Develop and implement testing procedures for electrical systems to ensure reliability
  • Diagnose and troubleshoot electrical issues across various systems
  • Prepare comprehensive bills of materials (BOM) for electrical components
  • Integrate IoT modules and power management units into designs
  • Assess safety and operational requirements to ensure compliance with industry standards

Candidate Profile

  • Holding a degree in Electrical or Electronics Engineering
  • Possessing at least two years of relevant industry experience
  • Strong foundational knowledge in electrical and electronic principles
  • Expertise in AC power electronics and associated control circuitry
  • Ability to interpret engineering drawings and technical documents accurately
  • Experience working with industrial electrical standards and safety protocols
  • Proficient in CAD tools such as Altium Designer and Eplan for schematic development
  • Excellent communication skills to collaborate effectively within diverse teams
  • Strong analytical mindset with problem-solving capabilities for quick issue resolution
  • Comfortable managing multiple projects simultaneously and meeting deadlines
  • Proficiency in MS Office applications including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint

What We Offer

Our client provides a dynamic work environment that encourages innovation and professional growth. Employees benefit from flexible work arrangements, comprehensive health programs, and opportunities for continuous learning and development. Join a team committed to pushing technological boundaries and making impactful contributions to the industry.

ad.title: Technical Documentation Specialist for Engineering Lifecycle Support
ad.description:
Join Our Client as a Technical Documentation Expert in Engineering Systems

We are seeking a detail-oriented professional to oversee the creation and upkeep of comprehensive technical and logistical documents that underpin complex engineering projects, including robotics, vehicle systems, and defense technologies. Your role will be pivotal in ensuring all documentation remains precise, compliant, and aligned with operational needs throughout the system's lifecycle.

Key Responsibilities

  • Creating and Updating Technical Resources: Develop detailed user guides, maintenance manuals, technical reference materials, and standard operating procedures (SOPs). Convert engineering insights into clear, organized documentation that supports various stakeholders.
  • Supporting System Lifecycle Activities: Facilitate logistics support functions such as planning maintenance schedules, managing spare parts and provisioning documents, and documenting system reliability and maintainability aspects. Ensure documentation remains relevant from deployment through decommissioning.
  • Collaborating Across Teams: Work closely with engineers from different disciplines, project coordinators, and operations teams to gather technical data and verify the accuracy of documentation.
  • Ensuring Compliance and Standards: Maintain documentation standards that meet client specifications, internal protocols, and regulatory requirements. Keep track of document versions and ensure traceability for audits and reviews.
  • Managing Document Systems: Oversee repositories to guarantee proper version control, timely updates, and stakeholder accessibility, supporting smooth audit processes and information retrieval.

Ideal Candidate Profile

  • Possesses a bachelor’s degree in engineering or a related technical discipline.
  • Has demonstrated experience in preparing logistics and technical documentation for complex systems.
  • Excels in technical writing with the ability to distill complex engineering concepts into understandable language.
  • Displays strong communication skills and the ability to coordinate with diverse teams effectively.
  • Highly organized with meticulous attention to detail and document management best practices.

What Our Client Offers

  • Opportunities to work on cutting-edge engineering projects with a focus on innovation and quality.
  • A collaborative environment that values continuous learning and professional growth.
  • Competitive compensation packages complemented by comprehensive benefits designed to support your well-being.
  • Flexible work arrangements to promote work-life balance and personal development.

ad.title: Senior Technical Project Lead for Financial Technology Solutions
ad.description:
About the Role

Our Client is seeking an experienced Project Lead to oversee complex, multi-department initiatives, ensuring seamless coordination with both internal teams and external partners. The successful candidate will be responsible for managing project schedules across various functions, driving progress toward key milestones, and maintaining clear communication with stakeholders about project status. This role demands a proactive approach to problem-solving, with a focus on delivering projects within scope, budget, and timeline constraints.

The ideal candidate will possess strong interpersonal skills, analytical capabilities, and experience working in highly regulated or tech-centric environments—such as financial markets, trading platforms, or SaaS solutions. The Project Lead will be instrumental in steering strategic and operational aspects of project execution, ensuring alignment with organizational goals.

Key Responsibilities

  • Develop comprehensive project plans, including detailed timelines and milestones, for multiple ongoing initiatives.
  • Coordinate efforts across departments such as Technology, Product Development, Operations, Legal, and Compliance to ensure cohesive progress.
  • Act as the main point of contact between our Client and external vendors, system integrators, and technology providers.
  • Monitor vendor performance, ensuring quality standards, adherence to SLAs, and contractual commitments are met.
  • Lead negotiations, resolve scope or timeline issues, and address technical or operational challenges through escalation when necessary.
  • Design and oversee User Acceptance Testing (UAT) plans, including test cases and acceptance criteria, in collaboration with technical teams.
  • Identify potential project risks and constraints early, proposing effective mitigation strategies to keep initiatives on track.
  • Maintain detailed documentation such as risk logs, issue trackers, and project records to support transparency and accountability.
  • Provide regular updates on project progress through dashboards, reports, and briefings tailored for executive audiences.
  • Ensure all projects adhere to governance standards, regulatory requirements, and internal audit protocols.

Qualifications & Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
  • Between 10 to 15 years of experience managing projects within financial technology, trading systems, or related sectors.
  • Proven track record working with vendors and cross-functional teams in complex, multi-stakeholder environments.
  • Deep understanding of project management frameworks such as Agile, Waterfall, or hybrid methodologies, with relevant certifications preferred.
  • Hands-on experience leading User Acceptance Testing cycles from planning to execution.
  • Exceptional communication, negotiation, and problem-solving skills to navigate diverse stakeholder needs.
  • Strong sense of ownership, disciplined follow-through, and operational rigor.
  • Leadership qualities capable of guiding teams and influencing stakeholders effectively to achieve project goals.

What We Offer

Our Client provides a dynamic work environment that encourages innovation and professional growth. Employees benefit from comprehensive health plans, flexible work arrangements, and opportunities for continuous learning. Join a forward-thinking organization committed to excellence in financial technology solutions.

ad.title: Senior Market Data Solutions Specialist (Singapore / London)
ad.description:
About the Role

Our Client is looking for an experienced and commercially savvy Market Data Program Expert to oversee the design, development, and strategic management of their market data services. This position involves shaping product offerings, optimizing revenue streams, and ensuring the technical robustness of data distribution systems. The ideal candidate will influence how market data is packaged, priced, and delivered to a global client base, working closely with technical teams to enhance the underlying architecture.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead the formulation and ongoing refinement of a comprehensive market data licensing framework, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and compliance standards.
  • Partner with legal teams to formalize licensing terms, update contractual conditions, and ensure enforceability across different jurisdictions.
  • Work with commercial units to develop and refine the financial structure of data licensing programs, including business planning and revenue modeling.
  • Draft, review, and update licensing agreements, data use policies, FAQs, and guidance documents for current and prospective licensees.
  • Serve as the main point of contact for licensees, managing application processes, data use declarations, and compliance documentation.
  • Negotiate licensing terms, usage rights, and data restrictions in collaboration with legal and compliance teams.
  • Coordinate cross-functional workflows involving legal, technology, and product teams to ensure timely license approval and data delivery.
  • Oversee the onboarding process for new licensees, including orientation on program requirements, data delivery procedures, and compliance standards.
  • Partner with technical teams to facilitate secure, accurate, and efficient delivery of licensed datasets, contributing to infrastructure planning and development.
  • Identify operational bottlenecks within licensing workflows, proposing improvements to enhance efficiency and accuracy.
  • Maintain comprehensive documentation on licensing policies, data products, and operational procedures.
  • Generate regular reports on licensing activity, operational performance, and compliance metrics to inform strategic decisions.
  • Engage with market participants such as trading firms, financial institutions, data vendors, and fintech companies to understand their data needs and foster partnerships.
  • Support sales teams with market data product positioning, client onboarding, and relationship management.
  • Act as a subject matter expert on data structures, licensing models, and delivery mechanisms, ensuring clients receive clear guidance and support.
  • Build and nurture long-term relationships with partners, clarifying expectations and delivering value through strategic collaborations.
  • Create outreach materials, capability summaries, and use-case frameworks to facilitate engagement and market expansion.

Ideal Candidate Profile

  • Possesses a bachelor’s degree in fields such as Computer Science, Engineering, Finance, Economics, or related disciplines.
  • Has between 5 to 10 years of experience in market data strategy, product management, or data infrastructure within financial markets or related sectors.
  • Extensive knowledge of derivatives markets, securities exchanges, or financial data providers.
  • Familiar with trading venues, clearinghouses, or fintech platforms, with a strong grasp of commodities derivatives and exchange data flows.

What We Offer

  • Opportunities to work in a dynamic, global environment with a focus on innovative data solutions.
  • Engagement with a diverse range of financial market participants and technology teams.
  • Supportive work culture that encourages professional growth and skill development.
  • Competitive compensation packages aligned with industry standards.
  • Flexible work arrangements to promote work-life balance.

ad.title: Senior Commodity Market & Product Strategy Specialist
ad.description:
About the Opportunity

Our Client is looking for a driven and detail-oriented professional to join their Product Innovation team as a Senior Commodity Market & Product Strategy Specialist. This position involves analyzing global commodity sectors and collaborating on the design and refinement of derivative instruments aimed at fostering market efficiency, enhancing liquidity, and supporting price discovery.

Key Responsibilities

  • Conduct comprehensive research on international commodity markets, emphasizing sectors such as precious metals, battery materials, liquefied natural gas, power, and environmental commodities.
  • Analyze both physical and derivatives markets, including OTC and listed products, focusing on supply-demand dynamics, seasonal trends, and macroeconomic influences to identify new product opportunities and support commercial strategies.
  • Track developments across various exchanges and monitor global market trends to uncover potential gaps and innovative product ideas.
  • Engage with clients and market participants to gather insights and feedback that inform product development and market strategies.
  • Assist in drafting detailed contract terms, covering delivery conditions, settlement procedures, contract sizes, tick values, and expiration structures, considering the full trade lifecycle.
  • Support compliance and risk management activities in collaboration with relevant teams to ensure market integrity and regulatory adherence.
  • Develop analytical reports, market commentaries, and dashboards to support sales, product teams, and strategic planning, with opportunities for external presentations if appropriate.

Ideal Candidate Profile

  • Possesses a bachelor’s degree in finance, economics, mathematics, engineering, statistics, or a related discipline.
  • Has over five years of experience in commodity markets, derivatives analysis, exchange operations, or market research roles.
  • Demonstrates a strong understanding of exchange-traded derivatives and commodity pricing mechanisms.
  • Familiar with exchange rules, contract specifications, and the process of product approval.
  • Knowledgeable about regulatory standards impacting derivatives markets, such as SFA, CEA, EMIR, or MiFID II.
  • Proficient in using market data platforms like Bloomberg, Refinitiv, or exchange analytics tools.
  • Possesses advanced skills in Excel and experience with analytical or programming languages such as Python, R, or SQL.
  • Capable of translating complex technical information into clear communication for diverse audiences.

What We Offer

Our Client provides a dynamic work environment with opportunities for professional growth, competitive compensation packages, and a collaborative team culture that values innovation and expertise. Employees benefit from flexible work arrangements, ongoing training programs, and a comprehensive benefits package designed to support their well-being and career development.

ad.title: Entry-Level Financial Operations Analyst – Exchange & Clearing Support
ad.description:
About Our Client

Our Client is a pioneering financial technology firm focused on creating a regulated commodities futures marketplace and clearing infrastructure in Singapore. They are also developing innovative software protocols and applications aimed at fostering trust and transparency in electronic trading environments. Established on the principles of efficiency, security, and seamless transaction flow, the organization strives to enhance market signals and provide advanced risk management tools to support the energy sector's transition. Committed to sustainability, they advocate for the classification and trading of commodities based on their environmental, social, and governance (ESG) impacts. Recognized by regulatory authorities as a Market Operator and Clearing House, they are a majority stakeholder in a publicly listed fintech company on the Canadian NEO Exchange.

Position Overview

We are seeking motivated recent graduates eager to start their careers in financial operations. As an Exchange and Clearing Operations Associate, you will play a vital role in maintaining the integrity and efficiency of trading activities within our client’s exchange and clearing systems. Your keen eye for detail, analytical mindset, and willingness to work in rotating shifts will be essential as you gain practical experience in the financial services industry.

Key Responsibilities

  • Operational Core: Support the core functions of the exchange and clearing operations by ensuring smooth, stable, and secure transaction processing to meet organizational goals.
  • Trade Monitoring: Assist in executing and overseeing trade, clearing, and settlement activities with precision to uphold accuracy and operational efficiency.
  • Regulatory Compliance: Develop a solid understanding of exchange and clearing rules, actively contributing to adherence and compliance efforts.
  • Data and Market Analysis: Leverage analytical skills to evaluate trading data and market trends, supporting the creation of detailed reports for decision-making.
  • Team and External Collaboration: Work closely with internal teams and external partners to ensure coordinated and effective operational workflows.
  • Standard Operating Procedures: Learn and follow established workflows, ensuring consistent application of controls and procedures.
  • Reporting and Documentation: Maintain accuracy and timeliness in all operational and compliance reports, supporting transparency and accountability.
  • Process Improvement: Identify opportunities to streamline procedures and enhance operational efficiency through innovative ideas.
  • Customer Interaction: Engage with market participants, addressing inquiries and resolving issues to support a positive trading environment.
  • Shift Work Support: Participate in a rotating shift schedule to ensure continuous coverage for 22/5 trading and clearing activities, with shift allowances provided where applicable.

Ideal Candidate Profile

  • Recent graduates holding a degree in Finance, Economics, or related disciplines.
  • Strong interest in financial markets and a desire to develop expertise in exchange and clearing operations.
  • Basic understanding of financial instruments, with enthusiasm for learning trading and settlement processes.
  • Excellent analytical capabilities, capable of quickly grasping complex data and concepts.
  • Proactive in understanding regulatory frameworks and ensuring compliance within financial markets.
  • Effective communicator with a team-oriented approach, adaptable to collaborative work environments.
  • Comfortable working in a shift-based schedule to support round-the-clock operations.

What We Offer

  • Competitive starting salary with opportunities for professional growth.
  • Comprehensive onboarding and mentorship programs led by seasoned industry experts.
  • Exposure to a vibrant, collaborative workplace that encourages learning and development.
  • Pathways for career progression within a forward-thinking organization.

ad.title: Senior Risk Analyst – Financial Markets & Derivatives
ad.description:
Join Our Client as a Senior Risk Analyst in the Financial Sector

Our Client is seeking a dedicated Risk Analyst to help oversee and refine their financial risk management strategies within Singapore. This role offers the chance to work with a vibrant team, engaging with a diverse array of financial products and tackling intricate risk challenges. It’s an excellent opportunity for professionals eager to expand their expertise across multiple facets of risk analysis and management.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist in designing and maintaining a comprehensive risk management framework, ensuring compliance with internal policies and evolving regulatory standards.
  • Perform daily risk control activities such as margin calculations, mark-to-market valuations, and generating risk reports, collaborating closely with Clearing Operations teams to mitigate clearing risks.
  • Investigate instances of margin breaches or risk limit exceedances, conducting root cause analyses, communicating with members, and managing escalation procedures, all while maintaining detailed audit records.
  • Support the calculation and oversight of option margin requirements using portfolio margining techniques like SPAN and VaR, reviewing volatility surfaces, stress scenarios, and option Greeks for complex positions.
  • Manage non-cash collateral assets, including government securities, letters of credit, and emerging digital assets, while evaluating collateral valuation methods and operational procedures such as digital token transfers.
  • Monitor collateral sufficiency and concentration limits across clearing members, ensuring daily reconciliation of posted collateral against margin needs and guarantee fund contributions.
  • Develop and maintain reports for management, regulatory bodies, and members, providing insights into risk exposures and operational metrics.
  • Stay informed on market developments and geopolitical events that could influence risk profiles, enabling proactive risk mitigation and opportunity identification.
  • Collaborate with teams across Clearing, Exchange Operations, Compliance, and Finance to ensure cohesive risk management practices.
  • Prepare documentation and reports for external stakeholders such as regulators, auditors, and independent validators, supporting transparency and compliance efforts.
  • Participate in onboarding new members, launching new products, and conducting periodic reviews of clearing participants, alongside conducting targeted market research on relevant risk topics.

Risk System Development & Analytics

  • Contribute to the enhancement of internal risk management tools by leveraging programming languages like Python, SQL, and cloud platforms such as AWS, supporting advanced analytics and automation.
  • Monitor and validate the performance of risk models, ensuring data accuracy and model appropriateness for ongoing risk assessments.
  • Engage in stress testing exercises, including reverse stress tests and sensitivity analyses, to evaluate margin adequacy during extreme market conditions.
  • Develop and implement sophisticated analytics tools and visual dashboards to facilitate risk monitoring, analysis, and strategic decision-making.
  • Design and execute test cases for risk system upgrades and new development projects, ensuring robustness and reliability.

Fostering a Risk-Aware Culture

  • Support initiatives to embed a strong risk awareness culture within the organization through daily activities, training sessions, and awareness campaigns.
  • Promote governance standards and best practices to uphold high levels of risk oversight across all operational areas.

Qualifications & Skills

  • Academic background in Risk Management, Economics, Business, Mathematics, Engineering, or related fields.
  • Proficiency in Excel VBA, SQL, Python, and Microsoft Office tools.
  • Understanding of derivatives, including futures and options, along with margin calculation methods and collateral management principles.
  • Strong attention to detail with a focus on precise process execution.
  • Interest in financial markets, commodities, energy sectors, and derivative instruments.
  • Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment that values proactive risk identification.
  • Excellent communication skills, with the capacity to work effectively across different teams and stakeholders.
  • Self-motivated with a proactive approach to process improvement and adaptability to change.
  • Previous experience in risk analysis, data management, or related roles within trading firms, fund managers, or clearinghouses is advantageous.

ad.title: Entry-Level Data & Business Intelligence Analyst for Financial Markets
ad.description:
Join Our Client as a Junior Data & Business Intelligence Specialist

Our Client is seeking a proactive recent graduate to fill a foundational role in data analysis and business intelligence within their Singapore-based team. This position is ideal for individuals passionate about financial markets, data-driven insights, and process automation.

  • Engage in analyzing data to generate reports, support operational decision-making, and create visual dashboards for internal teams.
  • Assist in developing automated data collection methods from various internal and external sources, aiming to streamline workflows and minimize manual tasks.
  • Collaborate with product development and business teams to design and interpret custom datasets, contributing to strategic projects and growth initiatives.
  • Support the documentation of business processes and data requirements under the mentorship of senior staff, ensuring clarity and accuracy.
  • Help map out data flows and business procedures from multiple origins to expand and refine data repositories.
  • Participate in testing and validating data pipelines and automation workflows to ensure reliability and efficiency.
  • Work closely with technical teams, including data engineers and IT specialists, to support ongoing projects and initiatives.
  • Develop a foundational understanding of derivatives trading and exchange operations, with opportunities for external engagement if desired.
  • Adhere to established data security and governance protocols to maintain integrity and confidentiality.

Desired Skills and Qualifications

  • Recent graduate or expected graduate with a degree in Finance, Economics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, or related fields.
  • Basic knowledge of derivatives, commodities, and fundamental asset pricing concepts.
  • Familiarity with data management principles, including databases, data quality, and datasets.
  • Experience with SQL, Python, R, or similar programming languages through coursework or internships.
  • Proficiency with Excel and data visualization or reporting tools.
  • Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, with a collaborative approach and entrepreneurial mindset.
  • Excellent analytical thinking and problem-solving abilities.

Join our Client to develop your expertise in financial data analysis and contribute to impactful projects in a dynamic environment.

ad.title: Senior Market Development Relationship Manager – Commodities & Derivatives
ad.description:
Overview

Our Client is seeking a seasoned professional to oversee and cultivate key partnerships within their Market Development Program, focusing on commodities derivatives markets. This role involves managing client interactions, tracking performance metrics, and ensuring alignment with program goals to enhance market liquidity.

Core Responsibilities

  • Serve as the main point of contact for designated Market Makers and liquidity partners, fostering long-term collaborations with trading firms, banks, and specialized market participants.
  • Develop a comprehensive understanding of the program's structure, including client trading strategies, operational needs, and risk profiles.
  • Collaborate with product teams, legal advisors, and marketing to introduce new initiatives and refine existing programs, aiming to boost market depth within budget and strategic parameters.
  • Lead the deployment of program updates, ensuring clear communication with participants and coordinating rule adjustments with legal teams before official implementation.
  • Monitor participant performance against established benchmarks such as spread limits, volume thresholds, and minimum trading requirements.
  • Gather market feedback to refine participation strategies, aligning client objectives with overall market quality standards.
  • In times of market volatility, work with internal teams to temporarily modify program parameters—such as widening spreads—to maintain liquidity, following stakeholder consultation.
  • Coordinate with internal departments—including Market Operations, Technology, and Legal—and external vendors to resolve client issues promptly.
  • Deepen knowledge of product contracts, exchange rules, order types, matching algorithms, and settlement processes to inform program development.
  • Provide insights on market structure, contract design, and incentive effectiveness to internal teams for continuous improvement.
  • Identify opportunities to expand market maker engagement across additional products or markets, supporting new launches and onboarding efforts.
  • Manage onboarding, certification, and technical readiness processes in collaboration with regulatory, legal, and operational teams, tracking participant enrollment and performance metrics.
  • Recommend infrastructure improvements to support liquidity growth and operational efficiency.
  • Assist in rule modifications, incentive adjustments, and trading enhancements aimed at optimizing market participation.
  • Generate regular reports on participant performance, incentive utilization, and liquidity metrics, providing strategic insights to senior management.
  • Contribute to liquidity strategies and client engagement plans through data analysis and market observations.

Qualifications & Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Business, Mathematics, or related fields.
  • Between 2 to 5 years of experience in client-facing roles within commodities or derivatives exchanges, trading firms, or investment banks.
  • Strong grasp of exchange-traded derivatives, market making operations, and trading workflows.
  • Proven relationship management skills with a focus on strategic partnership development.
  • Analytical capabilities to interpret trading data and liquidity trends effectively.
  • Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder engagement abilities.
  • Ability to thrive in fast-paced, dynamic trading environments.
  • Experience managing or participating in exchange market making programs is highly desirable.
  • Familiarity with incentive design, liquidity enhancement strategies, and market structure considerations.

Additional Benefits

Our Client offers a comprehensive benefits package designed to support your professional growth and well-being, including flexible work arrangements, ongoing training opportunities, and a collaborative work environment that values innovation and initiative.

ad.title: Senior Client Onboarding & Market Operations Specialist
ad.description:
Join Our Client as a Senior Client Onboarding & Market Operations Specialist

We are looking for a proactive, meticulous, and inquisitive professional to become part of our team. This role involves supporting clients and internal teams through various stages such as onboarding, compliance checks, and ongoing relationship management. Acting as a vital link between departments like Market Operations, Risk, Technology, and Commercial, you will ensure smooth resolution of client issues, maintain strong client and vendor relationships, and keep market support documentation aligned with industry standards and internal policies.

Key Responsibilities

  • Serve as the main contact for external clients during the onboarding process, facilitating communication and tracking progress across relevant internal units such as Risk, Technology, and Regulatory teams.
  • Assist in gathering and verifying necessary documentation and information to support client onboarding and due diligence activities, ensuring deadlines are consistently met.
  • Coordinate efforts to identify, investigate, and resolve client-related issues during onboarding and ongoing support, collaborating with internal teams as needed.
  • Contribute to ongoing relationship management by preparing updates, documentation, and responding to inquiries related to sales or market activities.
  • Maintain a comprehensive understanding of exchange and clearing procedures, rules, and policies, and how they influence client interactions.
  • Address internal and external questions regarding the operational and client-facing aspects of the framework, providing clear and accurate information.
  • Act as the primary liaison with technology vendors, managing daily communications related to client onboarding, market access, and support activities.
  • Coordinate vendor contributions, monitor timelines, and gather information necessary for onboarding and issue resolution, escalating issues to appropriate teams when required.
  • Develop and update key client-facing documents such as onboarding guides, FAQs, and internal process manuals, ensuring accuracy and compliance with policies.
  • Track onboarding progress meticulously, maintaining records of milestones, dependencies, and potential risks, and prepare regular status reports for internal stakeholders.
  • Support the development and upkeep of departmental infrastructure, including templates, project trackers, and workflow procedures, to enhance operational efficiency.
  • Identify areas for process improvement, propose solutions, and help implement changes to optimize client support workflows and internal operations.

Qualifications & Experience

  • A bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Business, Law, Computer Science, or a related discipline.
  • Between three to seven years of experience in client onboarding, client services, operations, or compliance within relevant financial institutions such as exchanges, clearinghouses, brokers, banks, or technology providers.
  • Solid understanding of derivatives markets, including the full trade lifecycle and participant interactions.
  • Knowledge of regulatory requirements like KYC and AML, and experience with onboarding procedures.
  • Experience in project management or process enhancement initiatives is highly desirable.

Additional Benefits

  • Engagement in innovative projects that shape market operations.
  • Supportive team culture that values continuous learning and development.
  • Competitive compensation packages aligned with industry standards.

ad.title: Senior Institutional Commodity Derivatives Sales Executive
ad.description:
Overview

Our Client is seeking an experienced professional to lead client engagement and expand trading activities within the commodity derivatives sector. This role involves promoting the exchange’s offerings across various regions, including India, Singapore, and the Middle East, while serving as the primary liaison between onshore and offshore clients on a global platform.

Core Responsibilities

  • Develop and implement targeted sales initiatives aimed at institutional trading entities to boost trading volumes and open interest in listed commodity derivatives within a competitive environment.
  • Build and nurture robust relationships with Indian firms operating from Singapore, Dubai, and other key markets, ensuring ongoing client satisfaction and engagement.
  • Conduct client visits, organize industry roadshows, and facilitate product workshops to deepen market understanding and foster new business opportunities.
  • Analyze client activity patterns, assess market share, and monitor competitors to identify potential growth avenues.
  • Gain insights into client hedging, trading, and investment strategies, offering tailored solutions utilizing listed derivatives.
  • Gather client feedback and share insights internally through platforms like HubSpot, while providing regular updates to senior management.
  • Educate clients on cross-border market access, trading procedures, clearing processes, and the strategic use of offshore contracts for risk management.
  • Participate in marketing initiatives such as webinars, industry events, and promotional campaigns to enhance the visibility of the exchange’s commodity offerings.
  • Collaborate with onboarding teams to ensure a smooth transition from client onboarding to active trading.
  • Prepare comprehensive business reviews, market intelligence reports, and strategic updates for regional and global leadership teams.

Qualifications & Experience

  • A bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Business Administration, Engineering, or a related discipline; advanced certifications like CFA or FRM are advantageous.
  • Between 5 to over 10 years of experience in commodity derivatives sales, preferably within exchanges, brokerage firms, banks, FCMs, or trading houses.
  • Deep knowledge of derivatives markets, including margining, clearing, and experience transitioning from OTC to futures trading with physical clients.
  • A proven history of establishing and maintaining relationships with institutional or corporate clients, consistently meeting or exceeding sales targets.
  • Familiarity with trading platforms, market data systems, and end-to-end workflows in derivatives trading environments.

Skills & Competencies

  • Proactive Approach: Demonstrates ownership of client portfolios and proactively contributes ideas to team initiatives for mutual growth.
  • Business Acumen: Possesses strong sales instincts, negotiation skills, and the ability to recognize and capitalize on new opportunities.
  • Market Knowledge: Well-versed in commodity market fundamentals, derivatives valuation, hedging techniques, and risk mitigation strategies.
  • Client-Centric Mindset: Skilled at understanding client operations, articulating value propositions, and delivering tailored solutions.
  • Effective Communicator: Excels in verbal and written communication, confidently engaging with senior stakeholders and industry audiences.
  • Data-Driven Decision Making: Comfortable leveraging analytics to monitor performance, identify trends, and inform strategic choices.
  • Team Collaboration: A cooperative team player capable of working across departments such as product development, technology, risk management, and operations.
  • Regulatory Insight: Knowledgeable about the legal and compliance landscape affecting derivatives trading and clearing in relevant jurisdictions.

ad.title: Data & Business Intelligence Analyst for Market Operations
ad.description:
Join Our Client as a Data & Business Intelligence Specialist

We are on the lookout for a skilled professional with a knack for data gathering, process streamlining, and insightful analysis to bolster the development of a centralized data platform and analytics team. This position bridges the gap between business growth, sales strategies, and technological innovation, focusing on creating meaningful data collections that drive organizational expansion.

The ideal candidate will be instrumental in establishing this emerging function by pinpointing data needs, enhancing data flow efficiency, and spearheading automation projects that boost accuracy, scalability, and operational effectiveness. These efforts will support sales, product innovation, and marketing initiatives across various exchange offerings.

Core Responsibilities

  • Transform business needs into detailed technical and functional specifications to facilitate implementation and continuous improvement, while generating actionable insights for business analysis.
  • Design and define data architectures, including schemas, quality standards, and collection methods, working closely with engineering teams throughout development cycles.
  • Identify opportunities to replace manual or semi-automated workflows with automated solutions to increase efficiency.
  • Develop dashboards and reports that showcase key data points, supporting sales and marketing teams in making data-driven decisions and creating presentations for internal and external stakeholders.
  • Collaborate with product teams to build custom datasets and participate in project analyses aligned with business development and strategic planning.
  • Establish procedures for routine data maintenance, ensuring ongoing development aligns with technological advancements.
  • Serve as a liaison between business users and technical teams, fostering clear communication and understanding.
  • Promote data literacy within teams by sharing current trends and best practices, cultivating a collaborative and innovative work environment.

Desired Skills and Experience

  • A degree in fields such as Computer Science, Engineering, Data Science, Mathematics, Finance, or related areas.
  • Between one and three years of experience in roles like data analyst, business analyst, or similar, or exceptional recent graduates with relevant skills.
  • Familiarity with financial and commodity markets, especially understanding price relationships and market fundamentals.
  • Knowledge of derivatives products, including market data, trade processes, margining, and risk assessment.
  • Proficiency in SQL and experience managing relational and analytical databases.
  • Understanding of data pipelines, ETL/ELT workflows, and automation strategies.
  • Ability to interpret technical documentation, APIs, and system architecture diagrams.
  • Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving capabilities, and effective communication skills.

What We Offer

Our client provides a dynamic work environment with opportunities for professional growth, flexible work arrangements, and a comprehensive benefits package designed to support your well-being and career development. Join a team committed to innovation and excellence, where your contributions directly impact business success.

ad.title: Business Development & Operations Associate in Precious Metals Sector
ad.description:
Overview

Our Client is venturing into the precious metals industry with a focus on innovation and growth. We are seeking a proactive and driven professional to join the team and help shape the company's future trajectory. This position involves supporting strategic business expansion, onboarding new clients, and managing daily operational activities. It offers a unique chance for someone with an entrepreneurial spirit to take ownership and make a meaningful impact within a dynamic startup environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist in expanding the company's offerings by supporting initiatives aimed at developing new products and services within the precious metals market.
  • Lead the client onboarding process from start to finish, acting as the main liaison and coordinating with internal teams to resolve any obstacles that arise.
  • Engage with clients regarding pricing strategies and market insights, helping to boost trading activity and strengthen relationships alongside the sales and leadership teams.
  • Prepare engaging presentation materials and detailed documentation for external partners, clients, and internal stakeholders.
  • Support daily operational functions such as trade execution, inventory management, and responding to customer inquiries as the business scales.
  • Participate in system testing and troubleshooting to ensure operational tools and platforms function seamlessly.
  • Contribute to additional tasks like data management, website content updates, research activities, or marketing support as needed.
  • Identify opportunities for process improvements and propose solutions to enhance operational efficiency and scalability.

Qualifications

  • A bachelor’s degree or higher qualification.
  • Strong organizational skills with meticulous attention to detail.
  • An entrepreneurial outlook with a demonstrated ability to take initiative and assume responsibility.
  • Resourcefulness and flexibility, thriving in a startup setting with a passion for solving problems.
  • A proactive attitude, eager to learn and embrace new challenges.
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a small, fast-paced team.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills.

Preferred but Not Essential

  • One to two years of experience related to commodities or physical metals sectors.
  • Basic coding knowledge (such as HTML, CSS, Python) to support website or system tasks.
  • Interest or familiarity with the precious metals industry (training provided).
  • Experience in business development or supporting new initiatives.
  • Background in documenting operational workflows or process optimization.
  • Availability to commence work immediately is advantageous.

ad.title: Hotel Front Office Supervisor
ad.description:
Key Responsibilities

  • Monitor housekeeping reports for discrepancies and implement corrective measures as needed.
  • Address security concerns by guiding guests reporting incidents or theft, and collaborating with security personnel on guest conduct issues.
  • Manage cash float levels in line with occupancy expectations, and approve rate adjustments, room changes, cash advances, and cheque payments following established procedures.
  • Lead training initiatives for front desk staff, including planning, organizing, and conducting on-the-job coaching sessions.
  • Perform periodic inspections of outlets in the absence of the Outlet Manager to ensure standards are maintained.
  • Closely monitor room inventory to maximize occupancy and revenue, working with sales teams to optimize group bookings.
  • Take responsibility for guest and staff evacuation procedures during emergencies, such as fires, in the absence of the Safety & Fire Coordinator.
  • Participate in emergency response training programs as required by law and safety regulations.
  • Complete any additional tasks assigned by supervisors with professionalism and dedication.

Candidate Profile

  • Hold a diploma or higher qualification in Hospitality, Tourism, or a related discipline.
  • Possess 2 to 3 years of supervisory experience in front office operations within the hospitality sector.
  • Be skilled in using hotel management software such as Opera PMS or similar systems.
  • Having relevant certifications in hospitality services, leadership, or operational excellence is advantageous.
  • Exhibit strong leadership qualities, effective communication skills, and adept problem-solving abilities.
  • Maintain a professional appearance and demeanor, demonstrating a service-oriented attitude.
  • Be flexible to work rotating shifts, including weekends and public holidays.

Additional Information

  • Enjoy comprehensive training programs aimed at enhancing leadership and operational skills.
  • Benefit from a supportive team atmosphere that encourages professional growth and development.
  • Engage in a role that offers diverse responsibilities, from guest relations to operational management.

ad.title: Senior Loyalty Program & CRM Marketing Specialist
ad.description:
Join Our Client as a Loyalty Program and CRM Marketing Expert

Our Client is seeking a proactive and detail-focused marketing professional to enhance and expand their member engagement initiatives. This role involves designing and implementing lifecycle marketing strategies, boosting customer participation, and supporting the financial success of the loyalty scheme.

  • Program Oversight and Development: Lead the planning and execution of loyalty initiatives aimed at increasing member numbers and encouraging repeat business. Regularly analyze key performance indicators such as new sign-ups, active participation, tier advancements, and revenue impact.
  • Operational Management: Oversee daily program activities, including member registration processes, benefits setup, and campaign configurations. Track points accumulation and redemption patterns, assisting in liability assessments and breakage analysis in collaboration with finance teams.
  • Performance Evaluation: Continuously assess program improvements through data analysis and competitive benchmarking to identify growth opportunities.
  • CRM and Lifecycle Campaigns: Design and refine automated customer journeys covering onboarding, pre-stay, post-stay, re-engagement, and tier upgrades. Implement targeted email and CRM initiatives with segmentation, personalization, and dynamic content strategies.
  • Testing and Optimization: Conduct A/B testing on various elements such as subject lines, creative assets, offers, and journey logic to enhance engagement rates and conversions. Ensure email deliverability and compliance with data privacy standards.
  • Campaign Planning and Member Engagement: Develop loyalty-focused campaigns to attract new members, promote points earning, and encourage redemptions. Collaborate with marketing, digital teams, and property partners to integrate loyalty messaging into broader promotional efforts. Support tactical promotions that motivate member activity while aligning with business goals.
  • Data Analysis and Reporting: Regularly monitor and report on member behaviors, revenue contributions, and campaign effectiveness using CRM tools, analytics platforms, and business intelligence dashboards. Maintain KPI tracking and ROI assessments.
  • Governance and Stakeholder Collaboration: Ensure data accuracy through routine audits and maintain comprehensive documentation related to loyalty operations. Assist in onboarding property teams to loyalty systems and processes. Coordinate with technical vendors or internal IT teams for troubleshooting and system improvements. Support other related duties with professionalism and diligence.

Ideal Candidate Profile: Holds a degree in Business, Marketing, Hospitality, or a related discipline, with 4 to 6 years of experience managing loyalty programs and marketing initiatives. Demonstrates hands-on expertise with CRM and marketing automation platforms, possesses strong operational discipline, and excels in copywriting and data analysis. Comfortable working across teams and managing multiple campaigns simultaneously.

Our Client offers a dynamic work environment with opportunities for professional growth, a comprehensive benefits package, and a collaborative team culture dedicated to innovation and excellence.

ad.title: Senior Treasury Analyst – Cash & Capital Management

Key Responsibilities

  • Contribute to the administration and enhancement of the Group’s Treasury Management System, troubleshooting issues and implementing improvements.
  • Implement hedging strategies to mitigate foreign exchange risks, monitoring market trends and economic indicators.
  • Stay informed on currency market fluctuations and conduct research on macroeconomic factors influencing financial markets.
  • Assist in capital planning for new projects and investment initiatives, working closely with senior management.

Ideal Candidate Profile

  • Possesses a degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
  • Has at least five years of relevant experience in treasury functions, including cash and capital management.
  • Detail-oriented with strong analytical skills and the ability to work independently.
  • Excellent organizational and communication abilities, with effective time management skills.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office applications; experience with Treasury Management Systems is essential.
  • Familiarity with Bloomberg or similar financial data platforms is a plus.

What We Offer

Benefits include generous leave policies, performance-based incentives, health coverage, and insurance plans. ad.title: Senior Infrastructure Operations Specialist
ad.description:

  • Develop and maintain comprehensive operational documentation, including runbooks, escalation procedures, and system reference guides.

Cybersecurity, Governance, and Business Continuity

  • Implement and monitor security measures such as endpoint protection, firewall configurations, vulnerability management, and privileged access controls.

  • Assist in backup operations, disaster recovery testing, and ensuring readiness for potential disruptions.

Vendor Relations and Project Assistance

  • Support the planning and implementation of infrastructure upgrades and technology refresh initiatives.

Candidate Profile and Qualifications

  • Between three to seven years of hands-on experience in infrastructure support, deployment, or operations, depending on the level.
  • Proficiency in networking, server management, cloud platforms, identity services, and security controls.

  • Certifications such as CCNA, CompTIA Network+/Security+, ITIL Foundation, or Microsoft Azure certifications are desirable.
  • Excellent documentation skills, analytical problem-solving abilities, and effective communication with stakeholders are essential.

Our Client offers a dynamic work environment with opportunities for professional growth, along with comprehensive benefits designed to support your well-being and career development.

ad.title: Customer Experience and Service Quality Specialist
ad.description:
Overview

Our Client is seeking a dedicated professional to lead initiatives aimed at elevating service standards across their portfolio. This role involves analyzing guest feedback, audit results, and operational data to identify areas for enhancement and implement strategic improvements. Collaboration with property teams is essential to ensure consistent service delivery and uphold brand integrity.

Key Responsibilities

  • Coordinate quarterly evaluations of service and brand standards across multiple locations, ensuring thoroughness and consistency.
  • Gather, interpret, and present audit data with actionable insights, supporting continuous improvement efforts.
  • Monitor progress on action plans, ensuring timely completion and accountability from property teams.
  • Identify common service challenges and recommend targeted solutions to address recurring issues.
  • Drive process enhancements by integrating digital tools and refining audit methodologies.
  • Collaborate with property teams to bolster service at critical customer interaction points, such as check-in, stay, and post-service recovery.
  • Support onboarding and engagement activities that promote a service-oriented culture aligned with company standards.
  • Contribute to initiatives aimed at fostering a positive service environment through campaigns and engagement programs.
  • Assist in designing and analyzing customer satisfaction surveys to gather meaningful insights.
  • Work closely with brand leaders to maintain and elevate the overall guest experience standards.
  • Manage participation in service excellence awards, overseeing submission processes and storytelling efforts.
  • Identify and nominate outstanding teams or initiatives for recognition programs, encouraging a culture of excellence.
  • Organize annual recognition events, coordinating logistics, communications, and stakeholder engagement.
  • Utilize recognition platforms to motivate teams and reinforce service excellence behaviors.
  • Analyze feedback, audit results, and survey data to detect trends and opportunities for improvement.
  • Translate insights into practical strategies to enhance service quality and operational efficiency.
  • Support cross-departmental projects aimed at elevating overall service standards and operational effectiveness.
  • Leverage digital analytics tools and AI technologies to streamline reporting and insight generation.
  • Perform additional duties as assigned, demonstrating professionalism and dedication.

Qualifications

  • Degree in Hospitality, Customer Service, or a related discipline.
  • 1 to 3 years of experience in customer experience, service quality, or hospitality sectors is preferred; entry-level candidates are encouraged to apply.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, including PowerPoint and Excel.
  • Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and develop actionable plans.
  • Experience with guest feedback platforms and survey tools is advantageous.
  • Background in event planning or project management is a plus.
  • Familiarity with AI tools to enhance reporting and operational workflows.
  • Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to influence stakeholders.
  • Highly organized with effective project coordination capabilities.
  • Proactive mindset with a strong sense of ownership and follow-through.
  • Ability to work collaboratively across teams and confidently engage with diverse stakeholders.
  • Passionate about delivering exceptional service and recognizing team achievements.

ad.title: Senior Financial Controller – Portfolio & Business Strategy Leadership
ad.description:
About the Role

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead and nurture a finance team based in Singapore while coordinating with external service providers across Europe and the UK.
  • Ensure adherence to statutory requirements and facilitate routine audits in multiple jurisdictions, including Singapore and Europe.
  • Identify opportunities to enhance financial workflows, systems, and internal controls to keep pace with a dynamic business environment.
  • Develop and present budgets and forecasts to executive leadership, supporting strategic decision-making.
  • Offer expert technical accounting guidance, especially concerning new mergers and acquisitions.
  • Mentor team members and foster collaboration with departments such as Business Development and Legal to align financial strategies with overall business goals.
  • Perform additional duties as assigned, maintaining a high standard of professionalism and diligence.

Business Development & Strategic Initiatives

  • Support ongoing efforts to set up overseas finance functions and streamline standard operating procedures.
  • Undertake any other responsibilities assigned by leadership with commitment and professionalism.

Candidate Profile

  • Academic background in Accounting, Finance, or a related field, complemented by a recognized professional qualification such as CA, CPA, or ACCA.
  • At least eight years of relevant experience in accounting and financial management, ideally including exposure to listed companies and Big 4 audit firms.
  • Proven ability to design and strengthen internal control frameworks.
  • Versatile in handling both hands-on tasks and supervisory responsibilities.
  • Excellent communication skills with a talent for managing stakeholder relationships effectively.

What We Offer

ad.title: Professional Housekeeping Specialist for Luxury Hospitality Settings
ad.description:
Join Our Client as a Housekeeping Associate in a Prestigious Hospitality Environment

  • Prior to guest arrivals, verify that all assigned rooms adhere to the highest standards of cleanliness and presentation.
  • Keep hallways, corridors, and common areas tidy, including carpets, wallpapers, furniture, lift landings, pantries, and service lifts, ensuring a consistently neat environment.

  • Regularly inspect safety features such as fire exits, extinguishers, and emergency routes, addressing potential hazards proactively.
  • Report any unusual activity, unoccupied 'Do Not Disturb' rooms, or misplaced items in accordance with standard operating procedures.
  • Adhere to all health and safety regulations mandated by local legislation, ensuring a secure working environment.

Candidate Profile

  • Flexible to work rotating shifts, including weekends and public holidays.

  • Capable of functioning effectively in fast-paced, high-pressure environments.

Available Locations

ad.title: Senior Investment & Strategy Manager – Real Estate & Private Capital
ad.description:
Key Responsibilities

  • Partner with various business units to shape and implement the organization’s strategic planning cycle, aligning growth objectives and capital distribution priorities into a comprehensive annual plan.
  • Collaborate closely with senior leadership and department heads to execute approved corporate strategies, supporting initiatives such as market entry feasibility studies, mergers and acquisitions, and strategic alliances.
  • Develop and deliver detailed reports and presentations on a monthly and quarterly basis to keep executive teams and boards informed, crafting compelling visuals to facilitate strategic decision-making.
  • Assist in raising capital by managing relationships with investors, overseeing the investor pipeline, and coordinating roadshows for new funds in sectors like hospitality and student housing.
  • Prepare marketing documents such as offering memoranda, teasers, and pitch decks, translating complex investment opportunities into engaging narratives for potential investors.
  • Maintain the Due Diligence Questionnaire (DDQ) database, coordinating with internal teams to ensure timely and accurate responses to investor inquiries.
  • Supervise the investor onboarding process, ensuring compliance with anti-money laundering and source of wealth checks through third-party service providers.
  • Oversee fund operations, including capital calls and distributions, to optimize cash flow and ensure accurate investor allocations.
  • Generate quarterly performance reports and newsletters to keep investors updated on fund results and strategic outlooks.
  • Conduct thorough reviews of financial models and materials submitted by business units, validating assumptions and ensuring alignment with investment strategies before approval.
  • Manage the investment committee process, ensuring all submissions are comprehensive, standardized, and meet institutional standards.
  • Build and maintain fund-level financial models to monitor overall returns, forecast carried interest, and assess the impact of new acquisitions on fund performance.
  • Perform additional duties as assigned, demonstrating professionalism and diligence in all tasks.

Qualifications & Skills

  • Academic background in Finance, Economics, Real Estate, or related disciplines; professional designations such as CFA or CA/CPA are preferred or in progress.
  • Between four and seven years of relevant experience in areas like investment banking, private equity, real estate fund management, or corporate strategy within a real estate development environment.
  • Proven track record in capital raising activities, including creating offering documents, managing due diligence, and engaging with limited partners, preferably in real estate sectors.
  • Exceptional proficiency in Excel, especially in analyzing large datasets and scenario modeling.
  • Self-driven with strong problem-solving skills and the ability to manage multiple projects efficiently.
  • Experience reviewing legal documents such as partnership agreements and loan contracts, with an understanding of commercial terms.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, capable of producing impactful presentations for senior stakeholders and investors.
  • Exposure to preparing board-level materials or familiarity with reporting cycles of publicly listed companies is advantageous.

Additional Benefits

  • Opportunity to work within a dynamic team focused on innovative real estate investment strategies.
  • Supportive environment that encourages professional growth and development.
  • Competitive compensation package complemented by comprehensive health and wellness programs.
  • Flexible work arrangements designed to promote work-life balance.
  • Access to a broad network of industry professionals and investors, fostering valuable connections.

ad.title: Technical Maintenance Specialist – Laundry & Equipment Operations
ad.description:
About the Opportunity

Our Client is a social enterprise committed to creating meaningful job opportunities for individuals from underserved backgrounds, embodying principles of compassion and community service. The organization provides essential services such as laundry, cleaning, and moving assistance, aiming to empower those who face challenges in traditional work settings.

Key Responsibilities

  • Ensure the proper functioning of laundry machinery, including tunnel washers, washer extractors, and dryers, through regular maintenance and inspections.
  • Diagnose and resolve issues with equipment that is not operating correctly, providing timely repairs to minimize downtime.
  • Respond effectively to repair requests by assessing problems and executing appropriate corrective actions.
  • Perform routine cleaning and preventive maintenance on machine ductwork and related components to sustain optimal performance.
  • Keep an organized inventory of tools and spare parts, ensuring all equipment is well-maintained and ready for use.

Candidate Profile

  • Possess at least a NITEC qualification in Electrical or Mechanical Engineering, or an equivalent credential.
  • Demonstrate solid mechanical skills, including troubleshooting and diagnostic capabilities.
  • Ability to identify issues accurately and carry out effective repairs to machinery.

What We Offer

Join a dedicated team that values your skills and commitment. Our client provides a supportive work environment with opportunities for skill development and growth, along with benefits designed to promote your well-being and job satisfaction.

ad.title: Hotel Front Office Supervisor - Guest Services & Operations Leadership
ad.description:
Overview

Our Client is seeking a dedicated Front Office Supervisor to oversee daily hotel operations, ensuring seamless service delivery and operational efficiency. This role involves supervising key front desk functions, maintaining guest satisfaction, and supporting team development.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead and coordinate activities across the front desk, including reception, reservations, cashiering, and guest luggage services, to ensure smooth operations.
  • Supervise staff performance, promote a professional image, and foster a positive work environment that reflects the company's standards.
  • Deliver courteous and prompt assistance to guests, addressing concerns with tact and professionalism, and managing check-in and check-out procedures efficiently.

  • Review daily messages and follow-up actions from the Logbook at the start of each shift to maintain communication flow.
  • Control access to keys, handle inquiries, reservations, and guest complaints, ensuring proper documentation and follow-up.
  • Oversee housekeeping reports, identify discrepancies, and implement corrective measures to uphold quality standards.
  • Manage security concerns by guiding guests reporting incidents, addressing behavioral issues, and liaising with security personnel.
  • Maintain financial accuracy by managing cash floats, authorizing rate adjustments, processing payments, and adhering to credit policies.
  • Design and execute training programs for front desk staff, including on-the-job coaching and performance assessments.
  • Perform routine inspections of outlets and guest areas, especially in the absence of the Outlet Manager, to ensure standards are met.
  • Optimize room inventory to maximize occupancy and revenue, working closely with sales teams to manage group bookings effectively.
  • Participate in emergency procedures, including staff and guest evacuation during fire drills or incidents, and complete required safety training.
  • Handle additional duties as assigned by supervisors, executing tasks with professionalism and attention to detail.

Qualifications & Skills

  • Diploma or higher in Hospitality, Tourism, or a related discipline.

  • Flexibility to work rotating shifts, including weekends and public holidays.
